rationale: Hard pass. The archetype's bright pastel palette (sky blue, green, light grey) and tactile jigsaw-piece-dragging hook are diametrically opposed to TheSphinx's dark, mystical-minimalist gold-on-black aesthetic and its voice/Q&A deduction loop — there is no puzzle-piece manipulation to showcase. Audience also skews toward casual relaxation-seekers (typical jigsaw demo: older, female-skewed, low-intensity) versus our intellectually-engaged 25-45 trivia adults. Don't adapt this archetype; if the team wants a "satisfaction" angle, build a bespoke variant around the dopamine moment of correctly guessing the celebrity.
